label: Watchlist
description: Saved watchlists and symbol selection for the active underlying in options and futures views.

0.1.note: WidgetErrorBoundary in widget-wrapper.tsx covers all grid widgets.

0.4.note: No any types or @ts-ignore in widget file.

0.5.note: Widget file has no lib/mocks/ imports; WatchlistPanel is a pure UI component with no mock imports.

0.6.note: options-data-context has no isLoading field; watchlists are synchronous mock data.

0.7.note: Empty state handled — WatchlistPanel uses activeList?.symbols ?? []; empty symbol list renders an empty ScrollArea with no rows — no crash.

0.8.note: Context throws if Provider missing (WidgetErrorBoundary); WatchlistPanel has no async error path.

0.9.note: No dead code or commented-out blocks in widget file.

l0.notes: 0.6/0.8 N/A — synchronous mock data, no async paths.

1.1.note: Widget reads ctx.watchlists, ctx.watchlistId, ctx.setWatchlistId, ctx.selectedWatchlistSymbolId, ctx.handleWatchlistSelect exclusively via useOptionsData(). No inline fetch or mock import.

1.2.note: options-data-context.tsx calls useGlobalScope() at line 130; globalScope.organizationIds used to build scopedWatchlists (lines 273-279).

1.3.note: Org filter — when organizationIds set and does not include 'odum', only crypto watchlists shown; tradfi-us excluded. Org change visibly changes available watchlist tabs.

1.4.note: clientIds not applicable to watchlist — symbol lists are not per-client filtered.

1.5.note: Strategy filter not applicable to watchlist symbol selection.

1.6.note: Empty organizationIds → scopedWatchlists === DEFAULT_WATCHLISTS (all lists shown).

1.7.note: DEFAULT_WATCHLISTS in lib/mocks/fixtures/options-futures-mock — reference data, tracked in knownIssues for future extraction.

l1.notes: Org scoping is the primary filtering mechanism — watchlists visible/hidden by org. All data via useOptionsData().

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab + f9f29ea. Re-audit only if entitlements change.

3.1.note: Mode relevance: live ✓ / paper ✓ / batch ✓ — watchlist symbol selection meaningful in all modes.

3.2.note: Context reads mode from useExecutionMode() at context line 129.

3.5.note: Paper mode: symbol selection identical — drives chain display for paper positions.

4.1.note: No dead onClick handlers in widget file; WatchlistPanel has functional onSelectSymbol and onListChange.

l4.notes: 4.3/4.4/4.7/4.8 require human walkthrough (symbol selection, list switching, editable watchlist adding).

5.1.note: Target strategies: all options and futures strategies on the options tab. Watchlist is the primary symbol selection entry point — drives asset which drives all downstream data.

7.1.note: No hardcoded hex/rgb/hsl colors in widget file.

7.2.note: No mock data imports in widget file.

7.3.note: Widget file is 20 lines; well within limits.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.6.note: No security anti-patterns in widget file.

7.7.note: No hooks or deps in widget file; pure prop pass-through render.

8.1.note: No text-[...] or fontSize= in widget file.

8.2.note: No arbitrary spacing tokens in widget file; min-h-[200px] in className is a sizing constraint, not a spacing anti-pattern.

8.3.note: Widget delegates entirely to WatchlistPanel from shared trading panel.

8.4.note: Agent grep: no bare div onClick, no img without alt in widget file. Human keyboard pass for WatchlistPanel symbol selection required.

l8.notes: Note: min-h-[200px] is a layout sizing constraint (minH for scroll area), not a typography/spacing token — acceptable per spec. 8.5 responsive resize requires human verification.

keepSeparate.rationale: Watchlist is a persistent sidebar widget that drives all downstream data; separate from chain for independent resizing and toggling (showWatchlist flag in control bar).

relationships.notes: Drives asset selection (via handleWatchlistSelect) which propagates to all options widgets via OptionsDataContext. (options-trade-panel + futures-trade-panel + options-scenario absorbed into their host widgets on 2026-04-22 WU-3.)

dataContract.notes: Watchlist symbol lists are user-configured static data. Prices shown in watchlist will stream via WebSocket in production. Currently synchronous mock data.

gaps: Watchlist editing (add/remove symbols) not persisted — editable prop is UI-only in current mock implementation
coverage.notes: Crypto watchlists (BTC, ETH, SOL, AVAX, MATIC) and TradFi watchlists (SPY, QQQ, SPX, NDX) pre-loaded. Org filter restricts to crypto-only for non-odum orgs.

knownIssues: DEFAULT_WATCHLISTS reference data lives in lib/mocks/fixtures/options-futures-mock — should be moved to lib/config/services/options.config.ts or lib/constants/options.ts.

findings.description: DEFAULT_WATCHLISTS (watchlist symbol definitions) defined in lib/mocks/fixtures/options-futures-mock and imported into options-data-context. Should be extracted to lib/config/services/options.config.ts or lib/constants/options.ts.
options-watchlist-widget.tsx — 0 direct user-visible strings in widget file; strings in WatchlistPanel sub-component
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21
